reason: Repeated useful negative findings appeared in Air Quality, Diamonds, Wine Quality, Dry Bean, and Batch 11, but pandas often explains the raw finding.
Batch 7, 9, 10, and 11 show repeatable baselines, metric stress, negative controls, and clear anti-hype value.
itsdangerous and pluggy produced real execution, but dependency variance and static/runtime pytest mismatch remain unresolved.
The recent program produced stronger value from executed datasets and repos than from source-only review.
Air Quality showed sentinel-aware value, but the current custom tools are less mature for temporal protocols than for split and metric checks.
Potentially useful, but under-executed in Batches 5-11 compared with dataset and benchmark work.
Install/test reproduction is externally useful, but it needs runtime evidence improvements before promotion.
The strongest repeated pattern is public dataset execution plus schema, split, metric, baseline, negative-control, and replay discipline.
